## [00:00 - 03:02]

There are three types of people. Two of these types, they will survive, they'll do okay, but they won't experience the level of thriving that the third category of person will truly experience in their lives. I've unfortunately been all three. Let's talk about it. The first type of human. This is what I call the showman and this was me early on in my career. This was the version of me that I thought to myself, if I just learned how to talk the talk, I could talk myself into any job, I could talk myself out of any situation. This is going to be great. I can afford doing all the hard work. And you know what's crazy? It worked for a while. It worked for a while, however, it wasn't long lived because people quickly worked out that, oh, okay, okay, I can talk the talk, but he can't walk the walk. And it sucked when they found that out because I then felt like a fraud. So then being a showman didn't work. So then I discovered the second type of person, which is what I call the technician. And because being a showman didn't work, I now over indexed on being the technician. I thought to myself, oh yeah, well I'm going to become so good with my craft. I'm going to become so good that I'm going to let my work speak for itself in your face. So I completely abandoned all my communication skills and abandoned the showman within and just purely focused on being the technician. And the reality was now that I could walk the walk, I no longer invested time into talking the talk, I became invisible as a technician. And people often took credit for my work. And more than this, I would often be invisible and just become a cog in the machine. And that sucked too. But luckily I learned from my experiences and even with a pea-sized brain, I still worked out that, okay, there's a third type of person that I can become. Damn it. This is what I call the crossbreed number three. And the crossbreed is when you are a 50% mix of showmen and 50% mix of the technician. And I believe this type of person thrives. And they do genuinely fall into the category of winner takes all. And they win in most situations. And I'll tell you why. It was with this simple mathematical formula that's going to be so basic, it's going to make you want to vomit and die. But this was the, on a back of a napkin math that I did that fully convinced me of going down the path of becoming a crossbreed. As a crossbreed, I collect points from both teams. So I could either, I could even be eight out of 10 with my technical skill, eight out of 10 with my communication skills, and I'd still beat you even if you are 10 out of 10 with your technical skills. Meaning, if I'm eight out of 10 with my technical skills and eight out of 10 with my communication skills, I'm still overall a 16 out of 10. A 16 out of 10 beats a 10 out of 10. This little simple mathematical equation I did in my head changed my life because that's, that's when I started to pursue being a crossbreed. And if you're sitting there right now and you can really relate to this and you are, you are a purebred technician, come join me for a free two hour masterclass and I'll teach you how to become a crossbreed. I'll help you level up your communication skills. I'll help you level up your showmanship. Being a crossbreed has completely changed my life and it can change

yours too. All you have to do is click the link in the bio and you can sign up and join me for the free two hour masterclass. My friends, it's not about team technician or team showman.
